title: Into the Forest
html:
The crowd behind us has opinions.

I can hear them sorting themselves out without looking. The loud man's voice, quieter now, stripped of the panic register, moving through the group, building something. A few other voices answering. The ones who need a structure will go to whoever offers one first. That is not cynicism. That is how groups work under pressure. Someone becomes the center and everyone else decides whether to orbit or drift.

I am not interested in being the center.

"We should go," I say.

Maya looks at the treeline. She still has the branch. She has been turning it slowly since the kappa left, not a nervous gesture. Checking the weight, finding the grip. I note that.

"Where?"

"Water first. High ground after."

She nods once and does not ask how I plan to find either. Good. People who ask how before you have started moving tend to slow things down.

Soo-Yun has her stick and her bag. She is watching the crowd the same way I was, not with fear but with attention. She catches me looking and straightens up like she has been waiting to be told something.

"Stay between us," I say. "Tell me if you hear anything I don't."

She nods. I don't explain the second part. Her ears are younger than mine by ten years and she has been calm since the sealed room. If she stays calm she is worth keeping alert.

We move into the trees.

The clearing empties behind us in pieces. I hear two or three other sets of footsteps following at distance. Not joining us. Just moving in the same direction because the treeline is the only direction. That is fine. Parallel motion is not alliance.

The forest floor is soft and uneven. Roots at angles you do not expect. The canopy is high enough that light gets through in patches, not columns. Scattered, moving slightly with what might be wind above the treetops. The undergrowth is the problem. Dense at ground level, the kind that hides distance and breaks sight lines at 4 or 5 meters. If something is moving parallel to us at that range I will not see it until it has already made a decision.

I slow my pace and widen my scan.

Soo-Yun's footsteps adjust without me saying anything. She reads pace. Another note filed.

Maya moves on my left side, a meter and a half out. She has not put the branch down. The cuts on her hands from the bark have dried to thin dark lines across her palms. She notices me checking.

"I'm fine."

"I know."

She looks at the machete on my belt. I had pushed it through the loop as a makeshift carry, handle forward. Not ideal but functional.

"You know how to use that?"

"Not well. You?"

"My father had a farm." Short pause. "I know how to clear brush."

More than I have. I note it as a positive variable and we keep moving.

I find a stream about 12 minutes in. Hear it before I see it, a low consistent sound under the forest noise, the kind that does not vary with the wind. I angle toward it.

The stream is narrow, half a meter across, running clear over flat rock with good visibility into the water. No movement under the surface I can see. I crouch and cup a handful. Cold, no obvious smell, no visible discoloration. That is the extent of what I can test with my hands. I do not drink it.

---

【"Water Source Located: Unverified"】

【"Tip: Drinking unverified water in tutorial zones carries risk. Purification recommended."】

---

So the system knows where I am, or it is reading what I look at. Both are worth keeping in mind.

I look at the notification and then I look at the stream. Purification means fire. Fire means I need something to burn and something to burn in, and I do not have either yet. I file water as a problem for later and stand.

"There's a stream. We note it and come back."

Neither of them argues. Maya pulls a small notebook and pen from her bag. She writes without stopping to think about what to write. I like that.

"You had that when this happened?"

"I was in a meeting," she says. "I always have my bag."

I pull up the HUD properly for the first time. I have been checking it in periphery since the kappa but not really reading it. Now I stop and read it.

Name: Eric. Health: 100/100. Stamina: 87/100, recovering. No class listed. No title. No skills. Inventory: Machete (Common). Status: Tutorial Chapter 1, Time Remaining 46:54:11.

There is a tab I have not opened. I open it.

---

【"Attributes"】

【"Strength: 8 | Agility: 9 | Endurance: 9 | Perception: 12 | Charisma: 10 | Intelligence: 11"】

【"Unassigned Points: 0"】

【"Note: Attributes update at level-up. Current Level: 1."】

---

Perception 12. The highest number on the list by a margin. I do not know what the scale is or what 12 means relative to other participants, but I know what it means in context. I have been using it since the sealed room without knowing I had it. The number is catching up to the behavior.

I close the tab.

"Anything useful?" Maya asks.

"Stats. Nothing I can spend yet."

"Mine says I have a class," she says. "Observer." She says it flat, like she has not decided what to do with it yet.

"What does it do?"

"The tooltip says I notice things other people miss." A short pause. "Which I already knew."

I look at her. The notebook. The way she marked the stream without being told to. The nod she gave the wall-sitter in the sealed room.

"That is useful."

She does not say anything to that. She is looking at Soo-Yun, who has crouched near a root to look at something on the ground.

"Kid."

Soo-Yun looks up. She is holding a round object the size of a tennis ball, greenish-white, with a faint seam running around the middle. Not a rock.

"I found it under the root," she says. "There are three more."

---

【"Item Found: Tutorial Cache (Hidden, Tier 1)"】

【"Contents: Firestarter x1, Water Purification Tablet x4, Rope (10m) x1, Dried Ration x2"】

【"Distributed to nearest eligible participant."】

---

The items appear. I feel the weight shift in my pockets before I look down. Firestarter is a small block of composite material, the kind that takes a spark. Tablets wrapped in foil, four of them. The rope coils into the side of a pack I did not have a moment ago and now do. The rations are hard and dense and smell faintly of nothing, which is the smell of food designed to have no smell.

I look at Soo-Yun.

"How did you know to look there?"

She thinks about it. 'The moss was wrong. Pressed down but the root was dry underneath, like something had been placed there recently.'

I want to check her HUD. I cannot. I file the question for later.

"Good eye," I say.

She stands and brushes her knees off. 'Thank you, sir Eric.'

Maya tears a page from her notebook and hands it to me without explanation. Cache location, stream location, direction markers. I fold it and put it in my pocket.

We find a defensible position about 20 minutes further in. Not high ground. The terrain does not offer that without more time than I want to spend moving. What it offers is a rock face about 4 meters high running roughly east-west, with a shallow overhang that cuts exposure from above and a clear sight line along the base in both directions for about 15 meters. The ground in front is open enough that nothing crosses it quietly. Three sides covered by formation or sight line. The back is rock.

Not perfect. It is what the forest is offering.

"Here."

I clear the ground under the overhang with the machete. Dead brush, loose branches. Enough space for three people to sit without touching. The soil here is drier, out of the direct canopy drip. I gather what wood looks dry, which is not much.

Maya sits and opens her notebook. She has been adding to it since we stopped. Not notes about us, from what I can see. Notes about the forest. The angles of the light, which direction the stream ran, whether the bird sounds changed when we moved through certain sections. I wonder what the Observer class is actually tracking.

Soo-Yun unpacks her school bag. Textbooks, useless. Pencil case. A folded rain jacket, not useless. A half-eaten packet of crackers. A small folding knife her parents probably do not know she carries.

I look at the knife. She looks at me looking.

"For camping," she says.

"Good."

I set the firestarter on a flat rock and check the purification tablets. Four tablets for an unknown duration. Enough for today if we are careful. I make a note to find more caches before the first 24 hours are up.

The rain jacket goes over Soo-Yun's bag to keep the contents dry. She watches me do it without comment.

The sounds of the other group reach us about 40 minutes after we settle. Not close. Maybe 200 meters northwest, moving parallel. Enough voices for at least 10 people. The loud man, probably, with his orbit built. They are loud enough that anything within range knows exactly where they are.

Maya looks up from her notebook.

"Should we—"

"No."

She waits.

"They have numbers," I say. "Numbers need feeding. They will make noise finding food and more noise arguing about it. If something in this forest hunts by sound, they are doing us a favor."

It is not a kind calculation. It is the accurate one. She goes back to her notebook.

Soo-Yun has not asked about the other group. She is eating one of her crackers in small pieces, making it last. She catches me watching and offers the packet.

I take one cracker. Maya takes one when offered. We do not discuss rationing. The action is the agreement.

The light through the canopy starts shifting around the 3-hour mark since tutorial start. Not sunset, still too much light for that. The angle changes, shadows lengthen east. Mid-afternoon, maybe, wherever this is. The system timer reads 44:21:07.

I take first watch without announcing it. Back against the rock, machete across my knees, sight lines covered. I think.

The kappa knew how to hold a weapon. The system has caches hidden in the environment. There are class assignments, attribute scores, timers. Someone built this. Not a god. Gods do not need tutorials, and tutorials are designed. They have objectives and rewards and endpoints. Whoever is running this is not improvising. They have a shape in mind for how this goes.

The question is whether the shape includes me surviving it.

Honest answer: probably some of us. Enough to keep it interesting. Not all.

The branch snaps. Left. Fifteen meters, maybe twenty. Not wind.

I am on my feet before I finish the thought. Maya comes up out of her sit fast. Soo-Yun goes still and quiet, which is correct.

I hold up one hand: wait.

The sound does not repeat. Something moved and stopped. Or moved and is now moving more carefully.

I watch the left sight line. The undergrowth at the edge of the clearing pushes once, slow, not wind, then goes still.

Something is there.

It is not moving toward us. It is watching.

I keep my hand on the machete and I do not move and I watch back.

Thirty seconds. A minute. The undergrowth does not move again.

Then, quiet as a breath changing, it is gone. Whatever it was has decided not to be there anymore.

I stay on my feet another two minutes before I sit.

'Not a kappa,' I think. 'Too quiet.'

I note the direction, the distance estimate, and the time. I do not tell Maya or Soo-Yun what I think it was. I do not know what I think it was. Saying something was watching us out loud serves no one right now.

I file it and keep watch.

44 hours left.
